description: A broad category of dough-based parcels or pieces that may be filled or unfilled and are commonly boiled, steamed, pan-fried, or fried. In many cuisines, dumplings contain meat, seafood, or vegetables wrapped in dough.
preparation_steps: Prepare the filling if making filled dumplings.
Fill and seal the wrappers or shape the dough.
Cook by boiling, steaming, pan-frying, or frying depending on the style.
Serve hot with sauce, broth, or side dishes.

nutrition.protein: Low to moderate; higher with meat or tofu
nutrition.fat: Low to moderate; higher if fried

health_advice.benefits: Can provide a balanced combination of starch, vegetables, and protein depending on the filling and cooking method.
health_advice.risks: Fried dumplings and salty dipping sauces can increase fat and sodium.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose steamed or boiled styles more often and include vegetable-heavy fillings.

dietary_notes.note: Dietary suitability depends heavily on the wrapper ingredients and the filling.

allergens: Soy in many fillings or sauces
